text_original: The sage has no invariable mind of his own; he makes the mind of the people his mind.
text_normalized: The sage has no invariable mind of his own; he makes the mind of the people his mind.

text_original: To those who are good (to me), I am good; and to those who are not good (to me), I am also good; - and thus (all) get to be good.
text_normalized: To those who are good (to me), I am good; and to those who are not good (to me), I am also good; - and thus (all) get to be good.

text_original: To those who are sincere (with me), I am sincere; and to those who are not sincere (with me), I am also sincere; - and thus (all) get to be sincere.
text_normalized: To those who are sincere (with me), I am sincere; and to those who are not sincere (with me), I am also sincere; - and thus (all) get to be sincere.

text_original: The sage has in the world an appearance of indecision, and keeps his mind in a state of indifference to all.
text_normalized: The sage has in the world an appearance of indecision, and keeps his mind in a state of indifference to all.

text_original: The people all keep their eyes and ears directed to him, and he deals with them all as his children.
text_normalized: The people all keep their eyes and ears directed to him, and he deals with them all as his children.
